mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a broad consumer demand contraction that reduces corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in HOUST and RSAFS fail to predict negative returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization approaching maximum levels, prompting profit margin expansion and positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth yields negative 5-day forward returns in over 55 percent of observations during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0 percent imposes high financing costs on leveraged firms and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP growth and falling industrial production signals inventory accumulation that precedes corporate earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-INDPRO divergence signal produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 36-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls expand household income and consumer demand, directly supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 200 indicate entrenched inflation that forces aggressive central bank tightening, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 200.
